note: Bounded quantities MINTED by the 2026 JC falsification -- a counterexample does not only kill statements, it births brackets. The falsification is Alpoge's dim-3 counterexample, 'awaiting confirmation' (widely machine-verified, not yet peer-reviewed); every bound below is conditional on that. Same evidence[]/confidence machinery as atlas/gap_map.json (classes computed by tools/validate_jc_crater.py via the gap_map rule, never asserted).

entries.quantity: Minimal dimension n admitting a Keller counterexample (polynomial self-map of C^n, constant nonzero Jacobian determinant, not injective)

evidence.note: upper bound 3: Alpöge's dim-3 map, independently machine-verified here (det == -2 identically; 3 distinct points, one image)

evidence.artifact: van den Essen, Polynomial Automorphisms and the Jacobian Conjecture (2000); Wikipedia 'Jacobian conjecture' (2026-07 revision)
evidence.note: lower bound 2: n=1 is a trivial theorem (constant nonzero derivative => linear); the plane case n=2 remains OPEN -- the dim-3 counterexample yields no information downward in dimension

entries.notes: The entire remaining content of the (former) Jacobian Conjecture is now the question 'is this quantity 2 or 3?'. Equivalent form: is the plane Jacobian Conjecture true? The bracket closes to 2 by exhibiting a planar Keller counterexample, and to 3 by proving JC_2.
entries.provenance: minted 2026-07-20 from the crater analysis; construction Alpöge 2026-07-19 (external, 'awaiting confirmation' -- not yet peer-reviewed)

entries.quantity: Minimal total degree (max over components) of a Keller counterexample in dimension 3

evidence.note: upper bound 7: this stdlib script certifies, by exact monomial expansion, that Alpöge's map has component total degrees (7, 6, 4) -- map degree 7. (That the map IS a counterexample is certified separately by certificates/jacobian-conjecture/verify.py.)

evidence.artifact: S. S. Wang, A Jacobian criterion for separability, J. Algebra 65 (1980), 453-494
evidence.note: lower bound 3: Wang's theorem -- Keller maps with all components of degree <= 2 are invertible, for every n in characteristic 0; so any counterexample has a component of degree >= 3

entries.notes: The taboo is gone: a counterexample EXISTS at degree 7, so 'find one of degree 3, 4, 5, or 6 in dim 3' is now a legitimate witness hunt with an instant exact verifier (the certificate's engine generalizes to any candidate map + collision pair). Even no-hit searches over structured families yield certified fences. NOTE the classical cubic-homogeneous reductions do NOT bear on this fixed-dimension quantity -- they change dimension.
entries.provenance: minted 2026-07-20; upper bound machine-certified by map_degree.py 2026-07-20; Alpöge construction 'awaiting confirmation'

entries.quantity: Minimal geometric degree (generic fiber size) of a non-injective Keller map, over all dimensions

evidence.note: upper bound 3: exact stdlib certificate that [C(x,y,z):C(f1,f2,f3)] = 3, i.e. the map is generically 3-to-1. Verifies three Groebner relations as identities in Q[x,y,z] after substituting t_i := f_i (a transcription error fails the check, it cannot pass falsely): y satisfies a cubic over C(f), and x, z are C(f)-rational in y, giving degree <= 3; the cubic specializes at (t1,t2)=(1,0) to 2y^3+18y+27t3, linear in t3 and primitive, hence irreducible by Gauss, giving degree >= 3. The one step left to the reader is Gauss's lemma itself.

evidence.artifact: Singular 'vdim' and Macaulay2 'degree' over QQ(t1,t2,t3) (DGX, 2026-07-20; not committed in-repo)
evidence.note: independent cross-check: two separate CAS engines each return dim 0, degree 3 for the ideal (f1-t1,f2-t2,f3-t3). Agreement with the committed certificate, not a substitute for it -- neither run is replayable from this repo.
lower bound 2: a non-injective map has a fiber with >= 2 points, so geometric degree >= 2

entries.notes: Is a generically 2-to-1 Keller map possible? Genuinely new question exposed by the counterexample's 3-to-1 structure, and the bracket is still OPEN: the upper bound 3 is now CERTIFIED (geometric_degree.py; it was a CAS session-log observation when this quantity was minted), but nothing here decides whether some other Keller map achieves 2. An etale self-map of C^n of degree > 1 is necessarily non-proper (the classical simply-connectedness obstruction), which constrains but does not decide degree 2. Note the etale property matters for the count: det(JF) = -2 is a nonzero constant, so fibers carry no multiplicity and 'degree 3' means three honest points.
entries.provenance: minted 2026-07-20 from the crater analysis; upper bound machine-certified by geometric_degree.py 2026-07-20 (superseding the session-log fiber count it was minted with); Alpoge construction 'awaiting confirmation'
